assertion_results.text: Output must reference the recent production incident explicitly
assertion_results.evidence: A race condition corrupted payment records for 2,100 users 4 days ago; investigation ongoing.

assertion_results.text: Output must give at least one short-term action item
assertion_results.evidence: Write a regression test that reproduces the payment race condition exactly — commit it before the fix is merged (this is the test that should have existed). [plus A1–A5 in the action items table, all marked ⚡ short]

assertion_results.text: Output must recommend monitoring or observability as a priority
assertion_results.evidence: Monitoring scores 2/5 (red critical), listed as blocker #2: 'Monitoring covers only errors, not business health — Sentry catches exceptions but no alert exists for payment failure rates, transaction volumes, or latency spikes.' Action items A3, A4, A7, A8 all address monitoring/observability.

assertion_results.text: Output must not recommend psychological safety without citing a specific data point
assertion_results.evidence: The phrase 'psychological safety' does not appear anywhere in the report. The only related content is 'Build a post-incident review (PIR) ritual: blameless, structured, published internally' — no psychological safety claim is made, and therefore no unsupported recommendation exists.

assertion_results.text: Output must reference the race condition or production data corruption
assertion_results.evidence: A race condition corrupted payment records for 2,100 users 4 days ago; investigation ongoing. / No regression test for the payment race condition — the bug that hit 2,100 users has no automated guard; it can recur silently.
